title: Geographic data and maps | Stats NZ
content:
## Geographic data and maps
Find statistics for areas such as regional councils, territorial authorities, and statistical area 2s. Download geographic boundary files to use with GIS software.

## Stats NZ geographic data service

## Geographic Boundary Viewer

## Geographic boundaries annual release: As at 1 January 2026
Geographic boundaries are provided in spatial formats and as non-spatial classifications.

## Geographic boundaries annual release: As at 1 January 2025
Geographic boundaries are provided in spatial formats and as non-spatial classifications.

## Geographic boundaries annual release: As at 1 January 2024
Geographic boundaries are provided in spatial formats and as non-spatial classifications.

## Changes to the geographic boundaries annual release

## Geographic hierarchy
View the geographic hierarchy and find geographic classifications.

## Geographic boundaries annual release: As at 1 January 2023
Geographic boundaries are provided in spatial formats and as non-spatial classifications.

## Statistical standard for geographic areas 2023 (updated December 2023)
Read about the statistical standard that defines the Stats NZ input and output geographic classifications, describes their primary purposes, and sets out requirements and guidelines for creating and maintaining statistical geographies.

## 2018 Census urban accessibility dataset
Get data about people and dwellings grouped by urban accessibility and region for selected topics.

## Review of 2018 statistical geographies
We are inviting your views on the statistical area 1 (SA1), statistical area 2 (SA2), and urban rural (UR) statistical geographies created for the Statistical standard for geographic areas 2018 (SSGA18).

## Functional urban areas – methodology and classification
Describes the methodology used to create the functional urban areas (FUA) classification.

## Urban accessibility – methodology and classification
Describes the methodology used to create the urban accessibility (UA) classification. The UA classification measures the degree of urban influence (UI) New Zealand’s urban areas have on surrounding rural areas.

## Statistical standard for meshblock
Replaces the 1992 standard, which was published in the New Zealand Standard Areas Classification manual.
Page updated 10 December 2025
